titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robbins Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Marguerite S. Witkop and Betsy H. Morris

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 22, 2020 at 10:48

did.abstract: Correspondence, notes, sermons, poems, diaries, and miscellanea of the Robbins family of Branford and Enfield, Connecticut. Philemon Robbins, 1709-1781, a minister, and Ammi Ruhamah Robbins, 1740-1813, are central family members.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the estate of Howard Chandler Robbins, 1942.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robbins Family Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: For biographical information, please consult the following citations or visit the repository.
Philemon Robbins: B.A., Harvard, 1729; ordained as a minister at Branford, Conn. in 1732 and remained there until his death.
Allibone's Dictionary of Authors, volume 2, page 1815.

Sibley, John Langdon. Harvard Graduates [Biographical sketches of graduates of Harvard university, in Cambridge, Massachusetts].
Volume VIII, pages 616-629.
Dexter, F. B. Yale Biographies and Annals . Volume 2, pages 424-428.

Ammi Ruhamah Robbins: B.A., Yale, 1760; ordained in Litchfield, Conn. in 1761; accepted a chaplaincy during the Revolutionary War; did missionary work in New York and Vermont, 1783, 1794-1796; prepared a number of young men for Yale College.
Dexter, F. B. Yale Biographies and Annals . Volume 2, pages 670-673.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, notes, sermons, poems, diaries, and miscellanea of the Robbins family of Branford and Enfield, Connecticut. Philemon Robbins, 1709-1781, a minister, and Ammi Ruhamah Robbins, 1740-1813, are central family members.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Notes on the life of Mrs. Anthony Whiting
"An unfortunate Mothers advice to her Daughters"
Notes on sermons by Rev. Cotton at Bristol [Mass., later R.I.]
Relation of his experiences read in the congregation when he was admitted into full communion
Two ALS to his father, Philemon Robbins
Two ALS to his daughter, Sarah (Robbins) Battell
"A brief Journal of some of the more remarkable Events in My Tour to Canada"
Journal of the Rev. Ammi R. Robbins... Northern Campaign of 1776 (published edition of the above journal)
"Western Tour-Journal" diary of missionary tours in Vermont and New York
ALS to his father, Philemon Robbins
Twelve ALS to his brother, Ammi R. Robbins
Robbins, Francis Le Baron (1787-) and James Watson Robbins (1782-)
ALS to their sister, Sarah (Robbins) Battell
ALS to this brother-in-law, Joseph Battell
ALS to his uncle, Ammi R. Robbins
"3rd Confession" [enclosed in TLS, 1926 Jan 26, from L.E. de Forest to H.C. Robbins]
ALS to his son, Ammi R. Robbins
Correspondence with the First Congregational Society
ALS to her mother, Elizabeth Robbins
ALS to her niece, Sarah (Robbins) Battell
"Resolutions of the Cons. respecting ardent spirits"
